title: Pinterest surges 15% after earnings beat as company posts strong guidance
text: Pinterest shares jumped after Q1 2026 earnings beat analyst estimates. The company reported earnings per share of $0.27, surpassing the analyst estimate of $0.23, and revenue was slightly over $1 billion, above the expected $966 million. Sales rose 18% year-over-year. Strong Q2 guidance and AI-driven shopping initiatives buoyed investor sentiment. The company expects second-quarter revenue in the range of $1.13 billion to $1.15 billion, higher than the $1.11 billion that Wall Street was projecting. AI assistants make Pinterest more interesting as an investment, turning it from just an image discovery platform into one where purchases are made, generating more revenue. The company recently acquired TV analytics platform tvScientific, which will expand its advertising efforts. At least seven sell-side firms raised their price targets, with PINS stock trading up 11% intraday to $23.
